paper.title_en: Steering a warp drive without exotic matter
paper.abstract_en: A useful warp drive must accelerate, yet every compact positive-energy construction is static or constant-velocity; accelerating ones need exotic matter, abandon the dominant energy condition (DEC), or are non-compact (no Bondi four-momentum to balance). We construct a compact positive-energy object driven by photon-rocket recoil on a tidally shielded flat cavity. Bondi--Sachs balance, read as propulsion: any asymptotically flat, confined dominant-energy drive changes Bondi four-momentum only by radiating to null infinity, so it cannot steer without radiating. A Kinnersley photon-rocket exterior with prescribed passenger worldtube fixes the thin shell, whose flat interior is a warp drive. Bulk energy conditions collapse to $n^2\ge0$; steering obeys the closed-form law $-\dot m\ge 3m|α|$, paid by Bondi mass loss, and both budget and kinematic ceiling survive a positive $Λ$ to all orders. Our result is existence: admissible shells exist at small acceleration about a static anchor (surface DEC for $2m/R<24/25$), and a slow fixed-axis burn assembles into one real-analytic spacetime, its constant-$u$ cuts characteristic (shell fixed order-by-order, not Cauchy-evolved); the rotating axis stays open. The acceleration-compactness frontier is bracketed by rigorous and closed-form bounds. Minimum-radiation steering is the gravitational-wave-silent Damour dipole, a hyperbolic geodesic with Tsiolkovsky constant $3$, universal in the news-silent class: the $3$ is the collimation penalty over a floor of $1$. The radiating equilibrium is linearly unstable (flux anti-damps the radial mode), yet growth $\sim$ luminosity is bounded over finite burns by the fuel budget. The drive is causal, subluminal, a reaction drive needing no exotic matter in the thin-shell idealization: steering a warp drive is a problem of energy budget, not negative energy.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文提出了在无奇异物质条件下实现可操控曲速驱动的理论构造，涉及广义相对论中的能量条件、邦迪质量损失和薄壳动力学，具有较高的理论深度和新颖性。
decision.title_zh: 无需奇异物质操控曲速驱动
decision.abstract_zh: 一个有用的曲速驱动必须加速，然而每一个紧致的正能量构造都是静态或匀速的；加速型需要奇异物质、放弃主能量条件（DEC），或者非紧致（没有邦迪四动量来平衡）。我们通过在潮汐屏蔽的平坦空腔上利用光子火箭反冲构造了一个紧致的正能量物体。邦迪-萨克斯平衡，解读为推进：任何渐近平坦、受约束的主能量驱动只能通过向零无穷辐射来改变邦迪四动量，因此它无法在不辐射的情况下操控。一个具有规定乘客世界管的金纳斯利光子火箭外部确定了薄壳，其平坦内部即为曲速驱动。体能量条件简化为 $n^2\ge0$；操控遵循闭式定律 $-\dot m\ge 3m|α|$，由邦迪质量损失支付，并且预算和运动学上限在正 $Λ$ 下成立至所有阶。我们的结果是存在性：在静态锚点附近小加速度下存在可容许的壳（对于 $2m/R<24/25$ 满足壳面 DEC），并且慢速定轴燃烧组装成一个实解析时空，其常 $u$ 截面为特征性（壳按阶固定，非柯西演化）；旋转轴保持开放。加速度-紧致性边界由严格和闭式界限限定。最小辐射操控是引力波静默的达穆尔偶极子，一条具有齐奥尔科夫斯基常数 $3$ 的双曲测地线，在新闻静默类中是普适的：$3$ 是在基线 $1$ 之上的准直惩罚。辐射平衡态是线性不稳定的（通量反阻尼径向模），但在有限燃烧中，增长 $\sim$ 光度受燃料预算约束。该驱动是因果的、亚光速的，是一种反应驱动，在薄壳理想化中无需奇异物质：操控曲速驱动是能量预算问题，而非负能量问题。
